Author: Text indicating the name(s) of the author(s), typically found near the beginning of a document.
Chapter: A major division of the document, often labeled with a number and title.
Equation: Symbols and numbers arranged to represent a mathematical concept.
Equation Number: Numerals used to uniquely identify equations.
Figure: Visual content such as graphs, diagrams, code or images.
Figure Caption: Text providing a description or explanation above or below a figure.
Footnote: Clarifications or additional details located at the bottom of a page.
List of Content Heading: Headings at the list of context text, identifying its purpose or content. This may also be called a list of figures.
List of Content Text: The detailed entries or points in a list. These often summarize all figures in the paper.
Page Number: Numerical indication of the current page.
Paragraph: Blocks of text separated by spacing or indentation.
Reference Text: Bibliographic information found typically in a reference section.
Section: Major subheadings documenting specific topics. This is typically represented using a single number (e.g. Section 3).
Subsection: Secondary subheadings under sections. This is typically represented using a number followed by a decimal point, followed by another number (e.g. Section 3.2).
Subsubsection: Specific subdivisions within subsections. This is typically represented using a number followed by a decimal point, followed by a number, followed by a decimal point, followed by another number (e.g. Section 3.2.4).
Table: Information organized in rows and columns.
Table Caption: Descriptive text above or below a table explaining its contents.
Table of Contents Text: Entries in the table of contents listing sections and their page numbers.
Title: The headline identifying the document's main subject.
